[[BLOCK:srt_85]]
Speaker 2: You.

[[BLOCK:srt_86]]
Speaker 2: Okay.

[[BLOCK:srt_87]]
Speaker 3: Just look at me.

[[BLOCK:srt_88]]
Speaker 3: My next question is: How does CGU Online help students balance career development, personal life, and academic growth?

[[BLOCK:srt_89]]
Speaker 1: Most of our students are working professionals.

[[BLOCK:srt_90]]
Speaker 1: They have full-time jobs during the day.

[[BLOCK:srt_91]]
Speaker 1: And many of them—probably most—also have families and all the responsibilities that come with them.

[[BLOCK:srt_92]]
Speaker 1: So we understand that for adults juggling work and family, education needs to fit into the flow of their lives.

[[BLOCK:srt_93]]
Speaker 1: Since the courses are delivered online, students can study on their own schedules. They might wait until the evening, after the kids are asleep, to sit down and work on their assignments. Or they may use the early morning hours, before the rest of the household wakes up, to get some studying done.

[[BLOCK:srt_94]]
Speaker 1: We know those moments are valuable, so we design our courses around their schedules.

[[BLOCK:srt_95]]
Speaker 3: That really is a learner-centered model.

[[BLOCK:srt_96]]
Speaker 3: Programs like AI map and Sid are also gaining popularity among students in China.
